uncovered_areas: RestoreFromCache (lines 785-815) — still 0%, mock bypasses JSON deserialization
ParseToolArguments JsonException catch (lines 445-448) — malformed JSON path

findings.issue: OnToolCall callback invocation still unverifiable in unit tests. Test proves 'doesn't crash' but not 'callback fires'. Coder documented this as a limitation.
findings.impact: Low — callback mechanism is structurally simple (goal call). Would require integration test to verify.
findings.suggestion: Accept as known limitation. Could add PLang integration test later.

findings.issue: RestoreFromCache (lines 785-815) at 0% — mock settings store returns Data directly, never exercises the JsonElement/Dictionary deserialization paths that occur in production (SQLite returns JSON).
findings.impact: Low — the cache store/restore roundtrip works end-to-end in tests, just through a different code path than production.
findings.suggestion: Would need a mock that returns JsonElement to exercise this path. Not blocking.

findings.issue: Exception catch paths (ParseToolArguments L445, ResolveImage L572, ParseApiResponse L751) at 0%. These are defensive catches for malformed data.
findings.impact: Low — defensive code for edge cases. If these threw, the error would bubble up naturally.
findings.suggestion: Not blocking. Add malformed JSON test for ParseToolArguments if desired.

summary: 1962 C# tests pass (0 failures). All 4 major findings from v2 resolved. Coverage improved to 87.6% line / 65.1% branch (+4.8% / +3.6%). 3 remaining minor findings: OnToolCall callback unverifiable in unit tests (documented limitation), RestoreFromCache mock-level gap, defensive exception paths at 0%. All non-blocking. Recommend proceeding to security analyst.
